body_txt: The Revenant is an older variety of zombie , originating in European folklore. In historic lore, the term "revenant" could be used more or less interchangeably for specters and corporeal undead. Ghost stories did not always strongly distinguish between these two concepts, and a given undead entity might not be specifically described as being one or the other. The most common defining trait was that a deceased person had returned from the grave to haunt the living or seek revenge for something, regardless of whether it could walk through walls or not. In modern fiction, which makes a stronger distinction between the two forms, revenants are usually assigned to the physical half of the undead medley and tend to be played as a type of zombie-like being with greater awareness, memory, and purpose than the common shambling corpse. Overall, revenants are less prone to rotting and falling apart than other sorts of zombies, in addition to normally retaining their intelligence and memories of their previous lives. They are driven by a single burning purpose, most often vengeance , true love, or some other Unfinished Business – driven by a desire so strong it can overcome even death. In some mythological circles, a revenant zombie can be brought back by things such as an improper burial or simply the person in question not realizing that they are dead and shouldn't be moving around . They may also be Living on Borrowed Time , in which case they'll have to race against a ticking clock to do whatever they stayed behind to do. While conceptually very old, and the prototype from which many other undead derive, the revenant has largely faded from modern works in favor of other, more modern breeds of zombie, as well as the bloodsucking vampire and the sorcerous lich . The exception is the Slasher Movie genre, in which most undead slashers are Revenant Zombies, though rarely named as such. Of note is the fact that, in contrast to other types of undead beings from across cultures and fiction, a revenant could be a fresh corpse, skeletal, or anything in between. When the character retains not only intelligence, but also good looks, they're an Attractive Zombie . May overlap with Friendly Zombie if they're benign and have good intentions, or Transhuman Treachery should they think of themselves as a different species from their living/healthy counterparts and/or willingly participate in To Serve Man / I'm a Humanitarian . See also Ghostly Goals , Unfinished Business , Purpose-Driven Immortality , and Murder Into Malevolence . Compare Vengeful Ghost , for a noncorporeal form of undead often driven by similar urges as a revenant.
